questions.prompt_text: An AI agent operating in a Retail Inventory Management system processes 19 tasks per minute. How many tasks will it complete in 4 hours, assuming zero downtime?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert hours to minutes: 4 hours × 60 minutes/hour = 240 minutes. Step 2: Multiply by task rate: 19 tasks/minute × 240 minutes = 4,560 tasks.

questions.prompt_text: In a Hospital Emergency Triage agentic pipeline, Tool A costs $0.05 per call and achieves 60% accuracy. Tool B costs $0.33 per call and achieves 74% accuracy. If the pipeline makes 8784 calls, what is the cost-per-accurate-result for each tool, and which tool is more cost-efficient?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Tool A: accurate_results_A = 8784 × (60 / 100) = 8784 × 0.60 = 5,270.4 accurate results. total_cost_A = 8784 × $0.05 = $439.20. cost_per_accurate_A = $439.20 / 5,270.4 ≈ $0.0833 per accurate result. Tool B: accurate_results_B = 8784 × (74 / 100) = 8784 × 0.74 = 6,500.16 accurate results. total_cost_B = 8784 × $0.33 = $2,898.72. cost_per_accurate_B = $2,898.72 / 6,500.16 ≈ $0.4460 per accurate result. Comparison: $0.0833 < $0.4460, so Tool A is more cost-efficient.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Tool A: ≈ $0.0833 per accurate result; Tool B: ≈ $0.4460 per accurate result. Tool A is more cost-efficient.

questions.prompt_text: A News Summarization Pipeline workflow requires 5 agents to each complete a sub-task. Each agent takes 5.2 seconds. If run sequentially, what is the total latency? If run in parallel (ignoring overhead), what is the total latency? What is the time saved by parallelization?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Sequential latency = NUM_AGENTS × AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 5 × 5.2 = 26.0 seconds. Parallel latency = AGENT_LATENCY_SECONDS = 5.2 seconds (all agents run simultaneously). Time saved = sequential_latency − parallel_latency = 26.0 − 5.2 = 20.8 seconds. Speedup factor = 26.0 / 5.2 = 5.0×.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Sequential latency: 26.0 seconds; Parallel latency: 5.2 seconds; Time saved: 20.8 seconds (5× speedup)

questions.prompt_text: An Academic Research Assistant RAG agent has a knowledge base of 4486 documents. A similarity threshold of 0.78 (on a 0–1 scale) filters retrieved results. In a test run, 485 documents exceeded the threshold. What percentage of the knowledge base was retrieved? If each retrieved document costs $0.029 to process, what is the total retrieval cost?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Retrieval percentage = (RETRIEVED_DOCS / TOTAL_DOCUMENTS) × 100 = (485 / 4486) × 100 ≈ 10.81%. Total retrieval cost = RETRIEVED_DOCS × COST_PER_DOC = 485 × $0.029 = $14.065.

questions.prompt_text: A Customer Support Automation agentic pipeline has 8 sequential steps. Each step has an independent success rate of 97%. What is the probability (as a percentage) that the entire pipeline completes without error? If 496 independent runs are executed, how many successful end-to-end completions are expected?

pre_computed_answer.steps: Step 1: Convert success rate to decimal: 97 / 100 = 0.97. Step 2: Pipeline success probability = 0.97^8. Compute: 0.97^2 = 0.9409; 0.97^4 = 0.9409^2 ≈ 0.88529281; 0.97^8 = 0.88529281^2 ≈ 0.78374336. Step 3: Pipeline success percentage = 0.78374336 × 100 ≈ 78.37%. Step 4: Expected successful runs = NUM_RUNS × pipeline_success_probability = 496 × 0.78374336 ≈ 388.74 runs.
pre_computed_answer.final_result: Pipeline success probability: ≈ 78.37%; Expected successful completions: ≈ 388.74 runs (out of 496)
